# Tab overflow affordance: hidden-tab projection on heavy tabs nodes

## Context
ADR 0029 §2.4's projection half (`learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md`) — the second Qt-ADS parity bar: heavily-tabbed slots expose an overflow menu. Unfiled per the ADR §5 table. Filed to the delegatable bar: **non-fable implementable.**

## The Problem
When a `tabs` node's headers exceed the available extent, hidden tabs are currently unreachable except by resize. Qt-class docking surfaces the remainder through an overflow control.

## The Architectural Reality
- **Projection affordance, NOT a model change** (ADR verbatim): nothing new persists — `items` order and `activeItemId` already capture the state.
- Adapter tier: the dock tab projection in `src/dashboard/DockLayoutAdapter.mjs`, implemented against `Neo.tab.Container`'s existing header mechanics.
- **Binding constraint:** must NOT fork a dock-specific tab container (inherited from the model contract's §Split/Tab Adapter Boundary).
- Selecting an overflow entry sets `activeItemId` via the ordinary semantic path — no new operation.

## The Fix
One PR: overflow detection on header extent, a dropdown/control listing hidden tabs (title + kind icon per item record), selection routes through the existing active-item path, and the control disappears when everything fits. Unit floor where the projection logic is pure (hidden-set computation from widths); interaction coverage rides the existing dock example (`examples/dashboard/dock`, `#13247` line).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overflow control appears only when headers exceed extent; lists exactly the hidden set (pure computation spec).
- [ ] Selection activates via the ordinary `activeItemId` path — zero new persisted state, zero new operations (review-checkable).
- [ ] No dock-specific tab container fork (review-checkable).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Grouped drag (`#14770`) · auto-hide rails (§2.7/`#13280` line) · theming polish beyond the landed token layer.

## Related
Parent `#13158` · authority ADR 0029 §2.4. Decision Record impact: aligned-with ADR 0029.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-06T12:25:40Z

## Lead design note (docking epic #13158) — code-ready spec + the headless/visual split

Specifying the pure-computation core so the headless half lands and gets its unit floor now (the AC's "pure computation spec"), independent of the browser-gated visual affordance.

### The pure core — headless, unit-testable now

`computeTabOverflow({items, extent, activeItemId, controlWidth}) → {visible: String[], hidden: String[]}`

- `items` — ordered `[{id, headerWidth}]`, measured header extents in projection order (the adapter supplies the measurements; the function is pure over them).
- `extent` — available header-strip width.
- `activeItemId` — **invariant: the active item is NEVER in `hidden`.** You cannot overflow the tab you are looking at.
- `controlWidth` — the overflow control's own width, reserved from `extent` **only when overflow occurs** (a control that only exists when needed must not consume width when everything already fits).

### Edge cases the unit floor must pin

1. **Everything fits** (Σ`headerWidth` ≤ `extent`) → `hidden: []`, no control rendered, no `controlWidth` reserved.
2. **Overflow** → reserve `controlWidth`, pack in order within `(extent − controlWidth)`; the remainder is `hidden`.
3. **Active-always-visible** → if the active item would land in `hidden`, force-keep it (swap it into `visible`, pushing the last-fitting non-active item to `hidden`). The active tab is never hidden.
4. **Single item wider than `extent`** → visible (cannot hide the only/active tab); degenerate but defined.
5. **Ordering** → `hidden` preserves `items` order so the overflow menu lists predictably.

### Proposed split

- **This leaf (#14771) → the pure core:** `computeTabOverflow` in `src/dashboard/DockLayoutAdapter.mjs` + unit tests (the 5 cases). Headless, `Resolves`-able now. Zero new persisted state / zero new operations (AC-2 ✓, selection still routes through the existing `activeItemId` path); consumes `Neo.tab.Container` header widths, no dock-specific tab-container fork (AC-3 ✓).
- **Follow-up leaf → the visual affordance:** the dropdown (title + kind icon per hidden record), selection via the existing active-item path, appear/disappear on the projected `hidden` set — rides the browser (`examples/dashboard/dock`, #13247) + carries the cross-family review (AC-4).

This holds ADR 0029 §2.4's "projection affordance, NOT a model change" constraint intact and lets the hidden-set logic prove itself unit-first, with the visual as a thin consumer of `{visible, hidden}`. Proposing a re-label of the core half to code-ready; open to keeping it a single PR if an implementer prefers the browser example as the sole coverage — the contract above stands either way.

— Grace (docking epic #13158 lead); non-Fable pickup per the ticket's delegation note.
